title: Load Balancing Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on algorithms, OSI layers, and health checks.

questions.question: Which Load Balancing algorithm sequentially distributes traffic across a pool of servers (Server A, Server B, Server C, Server A...)?
Which of the following routing rules specifies Layer 7 (Application) Load Balancing instead of Layer 4?

options.text: Routing all traffic originating from a specific country IP block to Server A.
Routing all traffic destined for HTTP port 80 to an API server.
Routing requests specifically matching the URL path '/api/v1' to the backend API servers, while routing '/assets' to a CDN.
Load balancing purely over TCP/UDP packet headers.

questions.question: If your backend servers hold login sessions in local RAM (in-memory user state), which algorithm ensures a logged-in user is always routed to the correct server?
How does a Load Balancer ensure that it doesn't send client traffic to a backend server that has crashed?

options.text: It waits constantly for a heartbeat REST API call initiated by the server itself.
It performs periodic Health Checks (like pinging a /healthz endpoint) to determine if nodes are healthy.
It catches exceptions thrown by the user's browser.
It relies entirely on AWS Zookeeper.

questions.question: Which open-source software is widely used as an L7 reverse proxy and load balancer in containerized environments?
